groups.readOnlyDescription: Rules and settings can be inspected, but editing and custom rules JSON sources are disabled.
groups.selectHint: Select a group on the right
groups.configUrlInvalid: Please enter a valid https URL.
groups.configUrlTypeHint: The rules URL must point to a .json file.
groups.sourceDialogDescription: Load and edit rules from another public GitHub JSON file without changing the instance settings.
groups.sourceDialogHint: The address is used for this editing session and must point to a public GitHub JSON file.
groups.duplicateKey: The key {key} appears more than once in {group}.
groups.invalidJson: The redirect config is not valid JSON.
groups.invalidRoot: The redirect config root must be a JSON object.
groups.confirmDelete:
Delete group “{label}”?
This will also delete its sub-groups and rules.

entries.description: Edit redirects rules in this group
entries.emptyTitle: No rules in this group yet
entries.emptyHint: Click the button to add one
entries.emptyReadOnlyHint: There are no rules to display in this group.
entries.newRuleDescription: Fill in the rule for “{group}” first. It will be added to the list after confirmation.
entries.newRulePathHint: The path cannot be empty. You can keep editing it in the rule list after saving.

editor.tipParse: Tip: Switching back to Rules will use the parsed content.

unsavedChanges.rulesDescription: Save the current rule changes before leaving. Discarding restores the last successfully loaded or saved rules.
unsavedChanges.settingsDescription: Save the current settings before leaving. Discarding restores the last successfully loaded or saved settings.

instanceConfig.title: Settings
instanceConfig.description: Manage public Runtime, analytics, access, and installed-plugin settings. Secret values remain in deployment environment variables.
instanceConfig.validationSummary: Some settings need attention before they can be saved.
instanceConfig.visualUnavailable: The current config.json cannot be represented safely as a form. Use the recovery editor below to repair it.
instanceConfig.recoveryTip: Recovery mode is only shown when config.json cannot be opened in the visual settings editor.
instanceConfig.fixedValue: This value is fixed by the installed plugin contract.
instanceConfig.noPluginSettings: This plugin does not expose editable public settings.
instanceConfig.disabledPluginSettings: Enable this plugin to edit its settings.
instanceConfig.disableRuntimePlatformConfirm: Disabling {name} will make the corresponding Runtime deployment unable to handle requests if that platform is already deployed. It may return 500 responses or a platform error page. If you do not plan to use this platform long-term, delete the corresponding platform deployment instead. Disable it?
instanceConfig.secretBindingHint: Enter the environment variable name, not its secret value.

runtime.title: Runtime
runtime.description: Configure the public Runtime origin, crawler policy, and remote-document cache lifetimes.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Configure the collector endpoint and the shared source namespace used by all Runtime adapters.

access.title: WebUI access
access.description: Choose who may enter the dashboard and which GitHub accounts may manage data.

plugins.title: Plugin settings
plugins.description: Enable installed plugins and edit their public options and environment-variable bindings.

fieldHints.canonicalOrigin: HTTPS origin used for QR codes and canonical Runtime links.
fieldHints.robotsPolicy: Allow or discourage compliant crawlers from indexing Runtime responses.
fieldHints.configCacheTtlSeconds: Seconds a Runtime may keep the last valid config.json before checking again.
fieldHints.redirectsCacheTtlSeconds: Seconds a Runtime may keep redirects.json before checking again.
fieldHints.ingestEndpoint: HTTPS endpoint that receives signed analytics events.
fieldHints.sourceId: Lowercase base hostname shared by all Runtime deployments.
fieldHints.accessMode: Public read-only still requires GitHub sign-in; listed managers may edit.
fieldHints.managerGitHubUserIds: Use numeric GitHub account IDs. Allowlist mode requires at least one manager.

options.publicReadonly: GitHub users read-only, managers can edit

routeEntry.noRuleItems: No rule items yet. Click the button below to add one.
routeEntry.typeTooltip:
prefix: Prefix matching
exact: Exact path matching
proxy: Reverse proxy
routeEntry.targetTooltip:
Supports target, to, url fields
Functionally identical
routeEntry.priorityTooltip:
Matching Priority
Smaller number = higher priority
routeEntry.analyticsIdTooltip: Stable identifier used to attribute requests after a path or target changes.
